title: cotula pygmaea
intro: Cotula pygmaea, also known as the buttonweed, is a small, annual herb that belongs to the daisy family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is said to have several health benefits, such as relieving digestive issues, reducing fever, and soothing skin irritations. Cotula pygmaea is often prepared as an infusion or tincture, which can be consumed internally or applied topically to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
